title: [agent-os] PM backlog synthesis and board stewardship loop
body:
## Objective
Make the PM/board steward loop able to keep the queue alive without waiting for a human to pre-create every issue.

## Current Reality
The `product-manager` command launches a GitHub-write Codex PM subprocess with a board snapshot. It can comment, label, and create issues through its prompt, but the backlog synthesis rules are not yet deterministic or smoke-tested.

## Acceptance Criteria
- PM prompt and docs clearly require roadmap-gap issue creation when the board is empty or stale.
- PM-created issues include role, priority, readiness, risk, validation, dependencies or sequencing notes, acceptance criteria, and stop conditions.
- A dry-run or fixture-backed check proves the PM launch receives enough board and roadmap context.
- Public-safety rules prevent local paths, secrets, raw logs, or transcripts in issue bodies.

## Validation
`python scripts/check_agent_os_config.py`, focused dry-run of `python scripts/agent_os_orchestrator.py product-manager --repo sabino/dxt --profile azure --model gpt-5.5 --dry-run`, and public-safety scan if docs/scripts change.

## Stop Condition
Stop before launching implementation workers; this issue is about queue stewardship.

title: [agent-os] Supervisor principal loop and merge queue automation
body:
## Objective
Evolve the main tmux Codex session into a principal engineer loop that chooses batches, monitors workers, converges PRs, and merges green changes under policy.

## Current Reality
`run --loop --merge-ready` can launch workers and merge green non-draft PRs, but it has no explicit merge queue, dependency graph, PR conflict model, or stale-worktree cleanup loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Define or implement a supervisor loop that reads open issues, active worker state, worktrees, open PRs, and CI checks before each batch.
- Detect branch/file overlap and dependency labels before launch or merge.
- Require rebase/repair sequencing when PRs conflict with main or each other.
- Merge only green PRs under the documented policy and summarize fan-in back to issues.

## Validation
`python scripts/agent_os_orchestrator.py status`, `python scripts/agent_os_orchestrator.py merge-ready --repo sabino/dxt` without `--apply`, targeted unit/smoke tests for any new script behavior, and `git diff --check`.

## Stop Condition
Do not run a large worker batch until the principal loop behavior is documented or implemented.

title: [agent-os] Multi-stage specialist pipeline for complex issues
body:
## Objective
Define and automate the PM/researcher/mapper/planner -> implementation worker -> reviewer/QA/auditor pipeline for complex dbt compatibility work.

## Current Reality
Roles and labels exist, but the orchestrator claims a ready issue directly for one role. Multi-stage handoff is currently manual through comments and labels.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Document the trigger for each stage and the issue comment block each role must leave.
- Add or plan routing rules that can split broad issues into child issues before implementation.
- Ensure implementation workers only start after reference map, owner modules, validation gates, and stop conditions are present.
- Ensure reviewer/auditor roles can run after PR publication without becoming mandatory merge gates unless the issue says so.

## Validation
`python scripts/check_agent_os_config.py`, dry-run routing examples, and docs public-safety scan.

## Stop Condition
Do not change product Zig behavior in this issue.

title: [agent-os] Project field reconciliation and dependency tracking
body:
## Objective
Make GitHub Projects reflect Agent OS state: role, status, track, validation, source grounding, branch, dependency, and readiness.

## Current Reality
`setup --apply-project --sync-project-items` can create/update the Project and add issue items, but live per-issue field reconciliation is not a full loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identify which Project fields can be updated from labels/comments without ambiguity.
- Add dependency representation using issue links, labels, or a documented comment convention.
- Provide a dry-run mode that reports field drift before applying writes.
- Keep all Project state public-safe and avoid local worktree paths.

## Validation
`gh project list --owner sabino --format json`, `python scripts/agent_os_orchestrator.py setup --repo sabino/dxt --apply-project --sync-project-items --dry-run` if supported or a documented dry-run alternative, and config checks.

## Stop Condition
Stop if GitHub Project API limitations require broad schema changes; write a narrower follow-up.

title: [agent-os] Worker lifecycle cleanup and stale claim reconciliation
body:
## Objective
Cleanly reconcile exited workers, stale `status:claimed` issues, merged branches, and leftover worktrees after PR fan-in.

## Current Reality
`status` marks dead processes as exited and `stop` can terminate active workers, but stale worktree removal and issue-claim cleanup are manual.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Report stale exited runs whose branches are merged or whose issues are closed.
- Safely remove only clean, merged worktrees after confirming no uncommitted work.
- Remove or update stale `status:claimed` labels when a worker exited without a live PR.
- Preserve long-running Codex parent processes and never kill unrelated process trees.

## Validation
Dry-run cleanup report, `git worktree list --porcelain`, `git status --short --branch` for candidate worktrees, and `python scripts/agent_os_orchestrator.py status`.

## Stop Condition
Do not delete any worktree unless the dry-run evidence proves it is clean and merged.

title: [supervisor] M2 Jinja and macro compatibility decomposition
body:
## Objective
Decompose M2 Jinja and macro compatibility into small source-grounded slices.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the supervisor or Jinja/macro specialist to create child slices, comment the slice plan, and stop before broad implementation if file ownership is unclear.

## Child Issue Plan
- Parse-time `execute=false` context boundary.
- Macro namespace search order and dispatch parity.
- Hook and hidden dependency recovery.

## Validation
Native Zig tests, focused pytest, and dbt oracle checks where CLI/artifacts change.

## Stop Condition
Stop if slices overlap the same compiler/Jinja/macro modules without PLAN.md sequencing.

title: [compat] YAML selectors and state/defer roadmap
body:
## dbt Behavior
Plan YAML selectors, state/result/source-status selectors, and defer semantics.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the selector/state specialist for a source-grounded reference map and next-slice proposal. It should not implement broad state/defer behavior in the first pass.

## Upstream References
Needs dbt Core v1 selector and state references plus Fusion parser/selector references.

## dxt Ownership
`src/project/selector.zig`, graph resolution, artifact inputs, and future state manifest handling.

## Validation
Native selector tests plus fixture-backed CLI tests. Stop before implementing broad state/defer behavior without a source map.

title: [artifact] Expand manifest/run_results/schema parity plan
body:
## Artifact Scope
Plan the next safe artifact-field expansion for `manifest.json` and `run_results.json`.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the artifact parity reviewer for a read-only reference map and field-risk review before any writer branch starts.

## Evidence Needed
Pinned dbt artifact schemas, dbt oracle slices, deterministic ordering, and volatile-field normalization.

## Stop Condition
Do not invent dbt fields or emit dxt-only metadata into dbt artifacts.

title: [compat] DuckDB execution semantics and failure continuation
body:
## Objective
Improve DuckDB execution semantics, failure continuation, and run-results parity without moving runtime behavior out of Zig.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the execution/adapter specialist, but the first pass should produce a small slice plan if the issue is still too broad.

## dxt Ownership
DuckDB boundary, build/test/run scheduling, run_results writer, and fixture ladder.

## Validation
Native tests where possible, focused pytest, and relevant public Jaffle build/run gates.

title: [research] Semantic layer and MetricFlow compatibility map
body:
## Research Question
What source-grounded slices are required for semantic models, metrics, saved queries, and semantic manifests?

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the semantic/cross-db planner for research and issue breakdown only.

## Expected Output
A public-safe `.agent/research/` note and PLAN.md update only if sequencing or risk changes.

## Stop Condition
No product implementation in this spike.

title: [research] Cross-database planner capability matrix
body:
## Research Question
Define a dbt-compatible cross-database planning direction using adapter capabilities, staging, pushdown, and movement policy.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the semantic/cross-db planner for research and issue breakdown only.

## Expected Output
Public-safe architecture note and issue breakdown.

## Stop Condition
Do not change dbt command semantics before dbt Core parity is stronger.

title: [ops] Release binary automation hardening
body:
## Change
Harden release packaging, checksums, version validation, and binary safety scans.

## Autonomous Starting Point
The local Agent OS orchestrator may assign this to the docs/release curator for a narrow release-hardening plan and, if safe, a focused docs/CI slice.

## Validation
Release workflow dry run, public-safety scan, runtime-boundary scan, and binary smoke checks.

## Stop Condition
Do not publish artifacts containing local paths, logs, caches, or private details.

title: [safety] Agent OS public-safety and runtime-boundary audit
body:
## Objective
Audit agent coordination docs, issue templates, scripts, and GitHub automation for path, secret, transcript, and Python runtime-boundary risks.

## Validation
`python scripts/check_agent_os_config.py`, `python scripts/check_runtime_boundary.py`, and `python scripts/check_public_safety.py`.

## Stop Condition
Block publication if tracked files include local absolute paths, secrets, raw logs, or session text.
